name: TransactionalEmailSend
concept: Single-recipient email queued via Save(): wraps SendEmailMessages() with one-element recipient list, persists EmailMessage to DB, optionally bypasses background worker via in-memory ISingleSendGridProcessingChannel when sendFast=true
trigger: Any system-initiated single email: password reset, user creation, approval notifications

observed_steps.step: 1. Caller invokes IEmailService.Save() with email parameters and optional sendFast flag
2. Save() delegates to SendEmailMessages() with single-element recipients list (wraps caller into multi-recipient path)
3. SendEmailMessages() builds EmailMessage: Status=Importing if hasAttachments else Queued; BccEmails applied only to first recipient (first=true flag)
4. _emailRepository.BulkInsertEmailMessages() persists all EmailMessage records to DB
5. If hasAttachments: _emailRepository.InsertEmailAttachments() + UpdateEmailMessages() transitions Status to Queued after attachment write
6. If sendFast=true: ISingleSendGridProcessingChannel.QueueMessages() pushes message IDs to in-memory channel, bypassing background worker polling

name: BulkEmailSend
concept: Multi-recipient email send: SendEmailMessages() called directly with IEnumerable<EmailRecipientDto>; BccEmails only applied to first recipient in list; all messages inserted in single BulkInsert call
trigger: Bulk broadcast email operations with multiple recipients

observed_steps.step: 1. Caller invokes SendEmailMessages() directly with multiple-element IEnumerable<EmailRecipientDto>
2. foreach loop with first=true tracking: BccEmails = first ? bccEmails : null — BCC only applied to first recipient; first = false after first iteration
3. Each recipient gets individual EmailMessage: Status=Importing (if hasAttachments) or Queued (no attachments); subject truncated to 200 chars
4. All EmailMessages persisted in one call: _emailRepository.BulkInsertEmailMessages(emailMessages)

name: TemplateEmailSend
concept: Template-based email construction: retrieves EmailTemplate by languageId and enum name, injects content into master template via $$MAIN_CONTENT$$, generates rendered HTML via IEmailContentGenerator, sends via SendEmailMessages
trigger: Broadcast receipt email sent after SmsGroup dispatch completes

observed_steps.step: 1. EmailSendBroadcastReceiptAsync() receives SmsGroup, Profile, sendToEmailsAndNames; resolves customer timezone and languageId
2. GetTemplateByNameEnum(languageId, EmailTemplateName.BroadcastReceipt) loads both content template and MasterTemplate from repository or embedded resource files

3. GetTemplateByNameEnum() injects content template into master template: masterTemplate.Html.Replace("$$MAIN_CONTENT$$", emailTemplate.Html); then replaces $$COMPANYNAME$$, $$PRODUCTNAME$$, $$BASEURL$$, $$SUPPORTMAIL$$ placeholders
4. IEmailContentGeneratorFactory.GetBroadcastEmailContentGenerator() selects generator based on SmsGroup.SendMethod (ByLevel, ByStdReceivers, ByMunicipality, etc.)
5. emailContentGenerator.GenerateHtmlForEmailAsync() produces rendered HTML body
6. If emailBody not null: SendEmailMessages() called with rendered body, template FromEmail/FromName/ReplyTo/BccEmails, category=BroadcastReceiptMails

name: SendGridGatewayDispatch
concept: Background gateway processor picks up queued EmailMessages and dispatches to SendGrid API; validates body/subject/email format; applies retry state machine on failure (up to 3 retries); stores X-Message-Id on success; updates EmailMessage status after every attempt
trigger: Background workload processor picks up queued EmailMessage batch

observed_steps.step: 1. ProcessWorkAsync() receives IReadOnlyCollection<IWorkload<EmailMessage>>; downloads blob attachments for SmsGroup files to local temp path
2. foreach workItem: calls ProcessEmailMessage(emailToSend, blobAttachmentsList); result assigned to workItem.NextStatus
3. ProcessEmailMessage(): if body or subject empty → Status=ValidationError, no send
4. Tools.ValidateEmails(mail.ToEmail): if invalid email format → Status=InvalidEmail, no send
5. _emailSender.SendEmailAsync() posts to SendGrid HTTP API with all email fields and attachments
6. On success (HTTP < 400): Status=SentToGateway (or SentToGatewaySandbox if testMode=true); XmessageId stored from "X-Message-Id" response header

7. On failure (HTTP >= 400): GetNextStatus() state machine transitions: SendingToGateway→QueuedFirstRetry→QueuedSecondRetry→QueuedThirdRetry→SentToGatewayFailed; PriorityOrder incremented; error response uploaded to blob storage
8. _emailRepository.UpdateEmailMessage() persists final Status and DateChangedUtc after every attempt

name: InboundEmailToSmsGateway
concept: Inbound email received via SendGrid webhook: parsed, whitelist-checked, deduplicated per Message-ID+recipient+profile, phone number extracted from recipient address, forwarded as SMS via IBroadcastSender
trigger: POST /Sendgrid/InboundParse from SendGrid inbound parse webhook
conflict_note: Step 6 (TryRegisterProcessedEmail) depends on ProcessedInboundEmail entity which has mapping_status=CONFLICTING: entity inherits BaseEntity with int Id, but DB schema has no Id column — composite PK on SHA2_256 computed columns. Steps 1-5 and 7-9 are fully verified. Conflict is isolated to the deduplication persistence mechanism.

observed_steps.step: 1. SendgridController.InboundParse() receives POST; parses multipart inbound email via WebhookParser.ParseInboundEmailWebhookAsync()
2. Text extraction: if no text → strip HTML to plain text via HtmlToPlainText(); truncate at ZWSP marker (\u200C) if present
3. Extract Message-ID header; null check on envelope.To and From; publish InboundParseEvent via IMediator.Publish()
4. InboundParseEventHandler.Handle() receives event; IEmail2SmsWhitelistService.GetMatchingEntry(notification.From) — if sender not whitelisted: log debug and return Task.CompletedTask (silent drop)
5. IProfileService.GetProfileById(whitelist.ProfileId) — if profile not found: log fatal and return Task.CompletedTask

6. foreach recipient in notification.To: TryRegisterProcessedEmail(messageId, to, profileId) — if already registered: skip (deduplication guard against multi-call webhooks) [CONFLICTING: ProcessedInboundEmail DB schema mismatch]
7. recipientRegex.Match(to): extracts phone number from recipient address format (+<digits>@<domain>) — if no match: log error and continue to next recipient

8. messageText = (notification.Subject + newline + notification.Text).Trim(); SetupMessage() constructs SendMessageSingleConfig with phone, message, profile; config.CodeOrigin = "InboundParseEventHandler"
9. _broadcastSender.SendMessageSingleGroup(config) — fire-and-forget via ContinueWith; SMS send errors logged but not re-thrown to caller

name: DeliveryStatusWebhookUpdate
concept: SendGrid delivery status webhook updates EmailMessage status in DB with no-downgrade rules per status type; successful updates where SmsLogId is present trigger cross-domain write to SmsLogStatuses (Email domain write to SmsLog domain)
trigger: POST /Sendgrid/SetEmailStatus from SendGrid event webhook (Delivered, Opened, SpamReport, Clicked, etc.)
cross_domain_side_effect: Email domain writes to SmsLog domain: _messageRepository.CreateSmsLogStatuses() called when updated EmailMessage has SmsLogId set

observed_steps.step: 1. SendgridController.SetEmailStatus() receives POST; JsonSerializer.DeserializeAsync<IEnumerable<SendGridRootEvent>>() parses JSON body
2. Each SendGridRootEvent mapped to UpdateEmailMessageStatusCommand: Id=x.EmailId, SmsLogId=x.SmsLogId, SgMessageId, Status=x.ConvertEventToEmailStatus()
3. UpdateEmailMessageStatusesAsync() groups UpdateEmailMessageStatusCommands by Status

4. No-downgrade rules applied per status: Opened/SpamReport/Clicked = AlwaysChangeStatuses (override anything); Delivered = cannot overwrite Opened; default = cannot overwrite Delivered/Opened/SpamReport
5. _emailRepository.UpdateEmailMessageStatuses(ids, status, overwritingStatuses) — returns IDs that were actually updated (skips records protected by no-downgrade rules)
6. For each updated ID: parse SmsLogId from command; if valid long: build CreateSmsLogStatusCommand with SmsLogId, StatusCode, Imported=false
7. If any smsLogUpdateStatuses: _messageRepository.CreateSmsLogStatuses() writes cross-domain status records to SmsLog domain
